choice: Seed the RNG deterministically from (Plan id + a hash of its inputs) so re-running an unchanged Plan reproduces identical percentile bands and probability-of-success, with an explicit 'reseed' action to draw a fresh sample. Persist the summarized output contract (ff44dae1) regardless; the seed lets us regenerate full paths on demand without storing 5,000 of them.
rationale: Actuals-vs-Plan tracking and scenario comparison consume the engine's output contract as a stable reference; if the Plan's success probability jittered by a point or two on every page load from fresh Monte Carlo noise, the comparison and user trust break. Deterministic seeding also bounds storage (seed + inputs vs. all paths) and makes regressions reproducible. The reseed escape hatch preserves the ability to sanity-check sampling stability when wanted.
